title: AMD Partners with Eric Trump-backed Foundation to Develop Humanoid Robots
description: AMD has partnered with an Eric Trump-backed foundation to develop humanoid robots, according to Reuters. The collaboration aims to leverage AMD's new X100 chip lineup, which features Zen 5 CPU and RDNA 3.5 GPU cores, as reported by Tom's Hardware. This development positions AMD as a competitor to Nv

sources.title: Eric Trump-backed Foundation partners with AMD to develop humanoid robots
AMD’s new X100 chip lineup puts Strix Halo into robots – APUs for physical AI bring Zen 5 CPU, RDNA 3.5 GPU cores to compete with Intel’s Panther Lake
Nvidia, Meet AMD, Your New Competition For Humanoid Robot Brains

sections.heading: Partnership Details
paragraphs: AMD has entered into a partnership with a foundation backed by Eric Trump to develop humanoid robots, as reported by Reuters.

sections.heading: AMD's X100 Chip Lineup
paragraphs: Tom's Hardware reports that AMD's new X100 chip lineup, which includes Zen 5 CPU and RDNA 3.5 GPU cores, will be utilized in the development of these robots.

sections.heading: Competition with Nvidia
paragraphs: Forbes notes that this move positions AMD as a new competitor to Nvidia in the market for humanoid robot brains.

faq.q: What is the significance of AMD's partnership with the Eric Trump-backed foundation?
faq.a: The partnership aims to develop humanoid robots, leveraging AMD's new X100 chip lineup, as reported by Reuters.

faq.q: How does AMD's new chip lineup compare to Nvidia's offerings?
faq.a: According to Forbes, AMD's entry into the humanoid robot brains market positions them as a new competitor to Nvidia.
